content: Welcome to my blog where I write about machine learning, systems, and projects.
title: My Blogs

content: Hello! My name is Michelle Bao I am a Data Science and Computer Science Double Major doing two internships and also a teaching for a club called Code for Good. My first internship is a startup with a start up company called Bluerobins where we focus on education and tech and helping students get the learning they need using AI. Additionally, I have a internship with the Citris lab at Berkeley where we are working on creating an AI Chatbot for their website and customizing emails to personalize it to people based on their interest area in health.
title: About Me

content:
Languages
Python Java C++ SQL JavaScript/TypeScript HTML/CSS Frameworks & Libraries

Git Regex Tableau Figma VS Code JSON OOP Company Role Year Description CITRIS and the Bantao Institute Data Science and AI Research Assistant Jan 2026 - Present Analyzing engagement data and project metadata to support development of a chatbot and content discovery tools for the CITRIS Health platform using NLP; implemented email scripts for personalized newsletters based on topic interest. BlueRobins AI/ML & Software Engineer Intern Jan 2026 - Present Built a robust backend pipeline leveraging the Claude API to transform unstructured curriculum PDFs into schema-compliant structured JSON powering an educational game platform. Delivered a working prototype backend demoed to 100+ students and investors; received the Best Storytelling Award at SkyDeck Startup Competition. RR/ID (Rapid Reviews and Infectious Disease) Lead Software/Data Intern Aug 2025 - Jan 2026 Led UX discovery with editors to identify workflow bottlenecks in the Quality Check Portal and translate needs into product improvements. Engineered a high-performance JavaScript frontend leveraging Airtable APIs to fetch and render 1,000+ columns of review data at scale, achieving a 20% improvement in operational efficiency. UC Berkeley CompSci 61A Academic Intern Jan 2025 - May 2025 Mentored 20+ students per week in Python, recursion, and OOP for one of the largest CS courses at Berkeley. Collaborated with faculty to revise lab content, addressing recurring learning gaps across multiple cohorts. Projects Role Year Description UC Berkeley Datathon for Social Good Dec 2025 Engineered NLP features across two institutional datasets totaling ~28000 rows, including text preprocessing and feature encoding; applied PCA and clustering to diagnose latent structure in missing data and reduced missingness by 30% via targeted imputation, improving dataset usability for downstream analysis. Built an interactive Tableau dashboard visualizing institutional clusters and enabling comparative analysis across colleges. Money Maker (Stock Forecasting) Project Lead Aug 2025 - Dec 2025 Contributed to a team-based full-stack stock forecasting application built with Flask, PostgreSQL, and scikit-learn. Developed a multi-modal classification pipeline leveraging news sentiment and historical market features; achieved 56% training accuracy and 43% test accuracy. Integrated Polygon API for market data ingestion and GDELT for large-scale news article retrieval; analyzed volume ratios and data coverage to evaluate signal quality and model reliability. Leadership Role Year Description Code For Good Head Instructor 2025-Present Led recruitment and curriculum development; taught AI/ML concepts in Python, including multiple linear regression, clustering, and practical use of AI APIs and embeddings. Robovinia, UA Robotics Club Founder & President 2021-2024 Launched the school’s first all-girls robotics team; coached programming (C++ and PID) and competition strategy. Honors/Awards Year NCWIT Aspirations in Computing – Rising Star 2024 2× Amaze Award, VEX Robotics World Championships 2022, 2023
title: Resume
